assertions.text: V1 was traveling east on the one-way exit ramp (implies V1 had forward travel speed eastbound prior to leaving the roadway).
V2, a tractor-trailer, was parked on the paved shoulder of the exit ramp facing east (implies V2 was stationary on the shoulder facing east before impact).
V1 departed the roadway to the left, then returned to the roadway, then departed the roadway to the right before impact (ordering of departures and return).
V1's front plane contacted the back plane of V2's trailer (V1 struck the rear of V2).
No braking is mentioned before impact (asserts that braking was not reported).
There was a single impact between V1 and V2's trailer (one distinct contact event reported).
V2 was pushed forward by the impact and came to rest near its pre-impact location (V2 moved forward and stopped near the impact point).
After impact, V1 rotated clockwise and bounced back, then came to rest on the left shoulder facing east (ordering of post-impact motion and final rest orientation).
The collision produced enough force to push V2 forward and cause V1 to rotate and bounce back (implies a non-minor impact).

verdicts.evidence: The EDR speed prior to impact was [98.0 km/h at t=-4.75 s](T2) and remained around [96.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), indicating V1 had eastbound forward travel speed before leaving the roadway.

The telemetry is for V1 only and contains no direct measurements of V2's motion or orientation; there are no numbers about V2 in the V1 data, so V2 being stationary and facing east before impact is [unverifiable](T1).

Lane departures and returns (left vs right) require lateral position or yaw trajectory of the vehicle; available channels (speed, steering, brake, delta_v) do not provide lateral displacement or heading to confirm the ordered departures and return, therefore this sequence is [unverifiable](T1).

Impact direction (front of V1 striking rear of V2) requires relative positioning or impact vector; the V1 telemetry shows a large [cumulative longitudinal delta-V of -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3) but does not identify whether the impact was to V1's front or which plane of V2 was struck, so the specific front-to-rear contact is [unverifiable](T3).

Brake channel shows it was ON at early pre-crash but transitioned OFF at [t=-2.75 s](T3) and remained OFF through impact at [t=0.0 s](T2), contradicting the assertion that braking occurred before impact being unmentioned; specifically, there was no brake application immediately prior to impact per the data showing [brake = 0 at t=0.0 s](T2).

Determining the number of distinct contacts requires positional or multiple crash-pulse signatures; the delta-V record shows a single cumulative crash pulse reaching [~ -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3) but cannot conclusively prove there was only one contact versus multiple quick contacts, so the count is [unverifiable](T3).

V1 telemetry does not include measurements of V2 position post-impact; while the large cumulative delta-V of V1 ([ -98.6 km/h peak](T3)) implies a strong impact, whether V2 was pushed forward and came to rest near impact cannot be determined from V1-only data and is therefore [unverifiable](T3).

Post-impact rotation direction and final resting location/orientation require yaw/heading or GPS track after impact; the V1 data contains a large steering spike at [t=0.0 s with steering = 40.5 deg](T2) and a large delta-V but no heading/GPS positions in the provided window, so the claimed clockwise rotation and final rest on left shoulder facing east are [unverifiable](T2).

The crash pulse shows a large cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching approximately [ -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3), which indicates a high-energy impact likely sufficient to move another vehicle and cause V1 to rotate and bounce back.

result.rationale: Telemetry from V1 shows high pre-impact speed and a large cumulative delta-V during the crash pulse ([98.0 km/h initial speed](T2), [final cumulative delta-V ~ -98.6 km/h](T3)), supporting that the impact was severe; however, many spatial details about V2 and left/right departures, impact plane and post-impact resting orientation are not available in V1-only sensor channels and are therefore unverifiable.

citations.claim: cumulative longitudinal delta-V of -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
